retry_reason: The refcount decrement (kfree_skb_reason) always succeeds—there is no failure path requiring retry.
contract_summary: This function drops an skb by releasing its reference via kfree_skb_reason. After the call, the skb must not be used. The return value is a netfilter verdict combining the error code with NF_STOLEN. The caller should return this value as the hook verdict. This is a put wrapper that consumes the skb reference; no further cleanup on the skb is needed by the caller.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to release a reference on an skb via kfree_skb_reason; the return value construction (err<<16 | NF_STOLEN) is trivial metadata for netfilter verdicts and does not change the fundamental refcount-release nature.

check.reason: kfree_skb_reason(skb, reason) decrements the refcount on skb->users.refs, which matches the operation on parameter 1 with access path skb.users.refs and type chain sk_buff--refcount_t--atomic_t. Segment count (3) equals type count (3).
